title: What an American thinks about Bolivia

transcript.text: Hello, this video is what an American thinks about Bolivia. Bolivia is a
Interesting place. Their capital city is inside of a really steep valley in the bottom of the
Valley and their main street is right over where the river is. So I guess they put the river below
The main street. The main street is right in the middle of the valley. And so when you walk away
From the main street, you're going straight up and it's really high. It's the highest city in the
World. Once you get over the rim, then it grows over the rim on the flat part. And that flat part
Is the same height as Potasi. So those two places are the highest cities in the world. They're like
13,000 feet. Unreal. And it's a huge megalopolis at 13,000 feet. It's insane. Because it's freezing
At night, even though it's right next to the equator or really near the equator. Yeah, Bolivians,
They're not that smart though. Because I ordered, my mom sent me some stuff and the guy and I live,
The hotel I was at was right next to downtown, like the easiest place you could possibly imagine.
Like F Street and G Street. Boom. You know, right in there in the hotel. And they couldn't find it.
And they like refused to bring it to me. I had to go down to the, to the UPS place,
Pick it up myself. And, um, yeah, the lady who ran the hotel when my light bulb died,
I had to pay for another one. She, she, she went out to buy it, but I had to pay for it.
I didn't really protest that much. She was really old. Um, but, uh, yeah, I saw some homeless people
There, which freaky because they, I mean, they, they're homeless and it's that freaking cold. I
Mean, they must die. I bet you some of them just die. I gave this old lady, my nice woolen jacket.
She was really old and like looked really not happy. So, and then like right in front of my
Hotel, there were these, um, witches and they call it the witches market cause they sell witch
Stuff like trinkets, magic trinkets and stuff. And including, um, Bobcat skin, which was only
Like 20 bucks. I was like, Oh my God. Um, yeah. Right next door is the highest, uh, ski resort in the
World too, which I rode my bike up one day. It's on a glacier. I guess the glacier has been
Shrinking every year, but, um, yeah, it's exciting that then you have, you can ride your bike over
This hill, this big pass, I guess the continental divide and then down this really steep road to
This town called something Potos, Coroico, the Coroico road. And that's just cliffs that go way down
Sheer cliffs. But, um, so they've got lots of cool stuff. You climb the mountain, Mount Patassi
From the top of the mountain, you can see like this, the big lake, like Titicaca and then the
Jungle on the other side. And then in the, and then all the other high mountains out there.
And, um, they have this huge solar, they've got a lot of stuff there that we've got, you know,
The high desert and then the high plains and then that lake and then, and then the jungle.
So, um, I heard there's like, there's black people in the jungle part. I didn't go there
Though, but, um, yeah, they're not very good cooks. I got a salad once and it was half of
It was, uh, onion and I could barely even eat it. It was killing my mouth with all of
The, you know, in my eyes with all the onion. That was the worst. I don't know what they were
Thinking, but I did see a cover band of Led Zeppelin. It was exactly like Led Zeppelin.
And they were really good. So I went to the dentist there, but the lady didn't know
What to do. She had to call the dentist and actually have the dentist tell her what to
Do. And then the dentist got on the phone with me and like, was like, Oh yeah, don't
Worry. She got it good. She's going to do this. I was like, Oh my God. Okay. And she
Did it. Okay. It was funny. Way better than the one in Ecuador who fucked up my teeth.
So that's for sure. Drill a hole in my teeth to put a cap on that fell out two, four months
Later. Yeah. I was there for the, for the strikes and all these, all the campesinos
Throw rocks in the streets and I had to like run up, run through, ride my bike through and
All, everybody was having to ride their bike that day cause there could be no, no, no cars.
But yeah, one old guy said he rode 60 miles there and back every day that week. That was
Obviously an exaggeration. Oh, I was talking to one guy, one campesino dude right there
In Copacabana town on the South edge of the lake. And he goes, he goes, what do you think
About America's drug laws? Cause he was all pissed off about how we like wanted to, one of our
Politicians in America wanted to fly over and like throw pesticides on all of the crops
Down there and to stop the Coca, Coca, Coca, cocaine cultivation. And I was like, he's
Like, that's fucking assholes. I was like, yeah, no shit. There are assholes. Um, but
I asked him about like the legends of the gods there and they were like, I think they were
Like, yeah, we believe that stuff. I guess how like Buracocha or something legend says that
He landed on Island of the sun and then came to the land. They had a Tiwanaku there. I didn't
Go though. I'm an idiot. I know. Dumbest thing I ever did. But, um, yeah, it's about that.
I didn't interact that much with locals, so I don't have that much to say. I climbed Potasi
And somebody stole my camera, which had my pictures of Potasi, one of the guys who were
Helping us. So, yeah. So that wasn't nice. Um, okay. That's what I think about Bolivia.
